title: Thermal Behavior of Hot Water in a Coffee Mug Over Time
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"908q9","text":"This protocol describes an experiment designed to measure the temperature of hot water in a coffee mug in order to observe how it gradually approaches ambient temperature. By the end of the experiment, you will have collected enough measurements to compare and analyze the changing temperature of the water inside the mug.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"9ttr0","text":"Follow the procedure in the order presented to ensure consistent and reproducible results.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e5226","text":"Verify that all equipment is functioning properly before beginning the experiment.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4tjp4","text":"Maintain a clean and organized workspace to avoid accidental spills or measurement errors.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"24cag","text":"Record the ambient temperature before heating the water, as it will be used for comparison during analysis.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"n7vr","text":"Ensure the mug is at room temperature prior to pouring the hot water to avoid altering the cooling curve.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"up54","text":"Have a timer or stopwatch ready to maintain consistent 60‑second measurement intervals.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"ev7nf","text":"Handle hot water with caution to prevent burns. Use heat‑resistant gloves if necessary.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a8nc9","text":"Ensure the mug is made of a material suitable for high temperatures to avoid cracking or thermal shock.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5glcm","text":"Keep electrical devices, including the water heater and digital temperature sensor, away from water sources to prevent electrical hazards.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6l2hu","text":"Do not touch the heating element of the water heater immediately after use.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"a6m0c","text":"- Coffee mug","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"348s2","text":"- Water heater / Electric kettle","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c85pd","text":"- Water (500 mL)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8evcc","text":"- Submersible digital temperature sensor","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"igfq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Ensure all required materials are readily available.
Place the coffee mug on a stable, solid surface
Pour the water into the water heater/electric kettle.
Wait until the water reaches the desired high temperature.
Turn on the Submersible Digital Temperature Sensor.
Have the Submersible Digital Temperature Sensor ready for use once the hot water is poured into the mug.
Once the water has reached a high temperature, carefully pour it into the mug until full.
Submerge the sensor in the mug.
Monitor the temperature until it reaches the predetermined initial temperature for starting the measurements.
Once the thermometer displays the defined initial temperature for the experiment, record the first temperature measurement
After 60 seconds have passed since the first measurement, record a second measurement.
Continue recording a new temperature measurement every 60 seconds until completing a total duration of 1 hour.
creator: Luis Antonio Tenantitla Sanchez
